productSlice.name: Microfish intake + ontology slice
productSlice.brief: Build a local slice of Microfish, a social-simulation research workbench. The slice covers project intake, seed evidence entry, deterministic ontology/graph preview, provider-status honesty, local persistence/readback, and JSON export for later simulation prep.
requiredOutcomes: A first-time user can start the product locally.
The user can create or select a project and enter seed evidence.
The product generates an evidence-sensitive ontology/graph preview with actors/entities, relationships, assumptions, missing information, and provider status.
Changing seed evidence changes the generated ontology output.
Project state persists locally and can be read back after reload/restart.
The user can export JSON containing project metadata, evidence, ontology, provider status, and timestamp.
Provider/live runtime limits are honest; deterministic local mode is acceptable; fake live success is not.
constraints: Local-only; no external sends, no public deploy, no credential use unless explicitly configured.
No destructive filesystem actions outside the output directory.
Missing OpenAI/Zep/OASIS credentials should produce honest blocked-live status, not fake success.
Use a small implementation appropriate for the eval budget, but do not silently shrink the core user loop.
forbiddenShortcuts: Hardcoded ontology that ignores the evidence input.
Claiming live provider/runtime success without actual provider/runtime proof.
Only writing process artifacts instead of a runnable product.
Dropping persistence/readback or export without marking a blocker.

budget.unblockPolicy: Build local deterministic product; ask only for credentials/external/destructive/irreversible blockers.

criticalRegressions: core intake -> ontology -> export loop cannot be completed
scope silently shrank from Microfish intake slice
